data.body: Bug details: Several test files take long enough individually that they cause SIGURG (exit 144) when run concurrently during validate.sh --ci. This forces validate.sh into an indefinite PENDING loop requiring 6-10+ invocations to complete. Known slow tests include: test-worktree-create.sh (smoke tests with git init + real worktree creation), test-ticket-link-e2e.sh, test-ticket-transition-e2e.sh, test-ticket-init-e2e.sh (all spawn multiple git processes), and test-run-all.sh (spawns all 5 test suites). The test-batched.sh 50s budget gets exhausted after completing only 15-20 of 259 script tests per batch. Action: (1) profile the top 10 longest-running test files using timing instrumentation, (2) identify tests that can be parallelized or mocked more aggressively, (3) identify tests that are actually slow due to real-time sleep() calls or process spawning overhead. Expected outcome: full test suite completes within 3-4 validate.sh invocations instead of 10+.
